rationale: Builds on the current best late quant-aware LR rebalance and tail FP16 rescue without replaying another scheduler tweak. The new idea is to make the final int8 roundtrip itself more faithful on biased matrix rows: large 2D tensors can now store a cheap per-row float16 offset when their row mean is materially nonzero, then quantize the centered residuals with the existing per-row scales. That directly targets final_int8_zlib_roundtrip_exact val_bpb at small byte cost, while also removing unused qmeta metadata and a redundant float-keep branch to stay lean under the 16MB cap.
title: Adaptive Row-Offset Int8 Quantizer
